sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports that the sock sticks to the wound. The patient reports a wound on their right foot. The patient reports foot pain. The patient reports a wound. The patient reports that he finished a course of antibiotics yesterday. The patient reports that he experienced a blister that worsened and developed into a wound.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's temperature is 98.1°F. The patient's vital signs are normal. The patient's heart rate is 72 respirations per minute. The patient's blood pressure is 110/65 mmHg. Examination of the right foot reveals a 1 x 2 inch circular wound on the dorsal aspect of the lateral right foot. The wound is approximately 3 weeks old. There is some yellow slough present with minimal granulation tissue. There is no surrounding erythema or cellulitis. There is no evidence of fluid collection. There is no necrosis. There is no odor. Vascular exam reveals palpable bilateral femoral and popliteal pulses. DP and PT pulses are present by Doppler. An X-ray of the right foot shows no evidence of osteomyelitis.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a problem with a diabetic foot ulcer. The patient's right foot presents with a wound that is not healing. The patient's diabetes is not well controlled.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am ordering an ankle brachial index (ABI) to determine the blood supply to the foot. I am also performing a debridement here in the office. I am prescribing a collagenase ointment to be applied to the wound once daily and covered with a dry sterile dressing. I want you to wear a surgical shoe to take pressure off of the area. I do not think any more antibiotics are needed at this time. I want to see you again in two weeks. I may refer you to a vascular specialist if the ABI indicates your blood supply is not optimal for wound healing. I want to get a diabetic consult to help modify your diet and bring down your hemoglobin A1c.
